REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SANDRA PIERCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PROPERLY ALLEGE HIS CAUSES OF ACTION IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 2.112. THE COMPLAINT, AS PLEAD, IS UNCERTAIN DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY. IT APPEARS, BUT IT IS NOT CLEAR, THAT PLAINTIFF IS ALLEGING AT LEAST 3 DISTINCT CONTRACTS PERTAINING TO (1) PROCEEDS FROM REFINANCE AND SALE OF PLAINTIFF'S REAL PROPERTY; (2) EARNING, E.G. PAYCHECKS; AND (3) MORTGAGE/TAX CONTRIBUTIONS TOWARDS DEFENDANT'S REAL PROPERTY. BREACH OF EACH CONTRACT IS TO BE SEPARATELY ALLEGED. FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION, IF ANY, ALSO TO BE SEPARATELY ALLEGED WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE SUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS NOT STARTED RUNNING UNTIL APRIL 2015; OR WAS TOLLED UNTIL THAT TIME. ADDITIONALLY, IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO PROCEED WITH THE CLAIMS PERTAINING TO REFINANCE/SALE PROCEEDS, PLAINTIFF SHALL ADDRESS THE SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION ISSUE RAISED BY DEFENDANT. $187,574.56 HELD BY DEFENDANT FOR PLAINTIFF (TO BE SPENT AT PLAINTIFF'S DIRECTION ONLY) IS THE PROPERTY OF THE BANKRUPTCY ESTATE PER 11 U.S.C. 541. PLAINTIFF HAD EQUITABLE INTEREST IN THIS SUM PER THE TERMS OF THE ORAL AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF MAY PETITION THE BANKRUPTCY COURT FOR PERMISSION TO PROCEED WITH THIS CLAIM. IN THE ALTERNATIVE, BANKRUPTCY TRUSTEE MAY SEEK LEAVE TO SUBSTITUTE IN AS A PLAINTIFF FOR THE CLAIM PERTAINING TO THIS SUM. IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PLAINTIFF MAY AMEND THE COMPLAINT OMITTING ANY CLAIMS PERTAINING TO REFINANCE/SALE PROCEEDS. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 90 DAY LEAVE TO AMEND IN ORDER TO ADDRESS THE MATTER WITH THE BANKRUPTCY COURT. THIS COURT DOES NOT MAKE A DETERMINATION REGARDING JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL ARGUMENT AT THIS TIME. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO: SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE BREACH UNDER CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2; ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO WITHSTAND JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL (SPECIFICALLY IGNORANCE, FRAUD OR MISTAKE) AND JURISDICTION ISSUES RAISED BY DEFENDANT ARISING OUT OF ALL CLAIMS TO ANY EARNINGS, INCOME, ASSETS, PROPERTY, OR CLAIMS PRIOR TO THE JANUARY 7, 2010 BANKRUPTCY FILING OR AMEND COMPLAINT TO OMIT ANY EARNINGS, INCOME, ASSETS, PROPERTY, OR CLAIMS PRIOR TO JANUARY 7, 2010 THAT WERE NOT LISTED IN THE BANKRUPTCY FILING. PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT. (CRC 3.1320(G)). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: MARIA OLOPERNES; NOT REPORTED; D501.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Advanced From Apr-29-19 To Apr-29-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. Tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to: Sufficiently allege breach under causes of action 1 and 2; Allege facts sufficient to withstand judicial estoppel (specifically ignorance, fraud or mistake) and jurisdiction issues raised by Defendant arising out of all claims to any earnings, income, assets, property, or claims prior to the January 7, 2010 bankruptcy filing OR amend complaint to omit any earnings, income, assets, property, or claims prior to January 7, 2010 that were not listed in the bankruptcy filing.
Plaintiff has 10 days to file amended complaint. (CRC 3.1320(g)). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b).

Judge Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Maria Olopernes; Not Reported; D501.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2018 Continued To Nov-27-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Sandra Pierce'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Anthony Bass, Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Sandra Pierce's Demurrer To Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly allege his causes of action in compliance with CRC 2.112. The Complaint, as plead, is uncertain due to Plaintiff's failure to comply. It appears, but it is not clear, that Plaintiff is alleging at least 3 distinct contracts pertaining to (1) proceeds from refinance and sale of Plaintiff's real property; (2) earning, e.g. paychecks; and (3) mortgage/tax contributions towards Defendant's real property. Breach of each contract is to be separately alleged. Fraud causes of action, if any, also to be separately alleged with requisite specificity. Allegations in the complaint are sufficient to show that the statute of limitations has not started running until April 2015; or was tolled until that time.
Additionally, if Plaintiff wishes to proceed with the claims pertaining to refinance/sale proceeds, Plaintiff shall address the subject matter jurisdiction issue raised by Defendant. $187,574.56 held by Defendant for Plaintiff (to be spent at Plaintiff's direction only) is the property of the bankruptcy estate per 11 U.S.C. 541. Plaintiff had equitable interest in this sum per the terms of the oral agreement. Plaintiff may petition the Bankruptcy Court for permission to proceed with this claim. In the alternative, Bankruptcy Trustee may seek leave to substitute in as a Plaintiff for the claim pertaining to this sum. In the alternative, Plaintiff may amend the Complaint omitting any claims pertaining to refinance/sale proceeds. Therefore, Plaintiff shall have 90 day leave to amend in order to address the matter with the Bankruptcy Court.
This Court does not make a determination regarding judicial estoppel argument at this time.
Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
